title: Are freedoms the same as pleasures/satisfactions?
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-as-philosophy-question from late June 2020: "Are freedoms the same as pleasures/satisfactions?" — followed by a six-item to-do list (personal training, Alexandria questions, e-commerce, vending machine uTurns, reading books, "start the my courses nigga").
  • Classic manifesto style note pattern: a heavy abstract question floats at the top while the body is mundane logistics — Yahir's signature structural move in this era.
  • uTurns is one of the earliest small-business / vending-machine ideas pre-BroMenu — slot it next to e-commerce and the recurring "earning-job" preoccupation that culminates in 2021 04 27 crypto internship earning.
  • The COVID pandemic era context is doing real work here: lockdown summer, no normal job, Yahir building a parallel curriculum (training + reading + courses + business ideas) instead of accepting the absence-of-school as freedom.
  • Foreshadows the adversity-as-freedom axis that becomes voluntary adversity: hints that Yahir doesn't equate "no constraints" with "satisfaction."

Source quote

"Are freedoms the same as pleasures/satisfactions?"

"-personal training? / -Alexandria questions and discussion / -e-commerce / -vending machine uTurns / -reading books / -start the my courses nigga"

Open questions

  • What were "the my courses"? Online business courses, school catch-up, something else?
  • Did uTurns (vending machine concept) ever get developed, or is this its only mention?
  • Is the answer-to-the-title-question implied anywhere later in the corpus, or did it stay open?

Wikilinks

covid pandemic era · alexandria nagy · voluntary adversity · bromenu · asking the right question · learn to run before flying · fear of not doing · 2021 04 27 crypto internship earning · grants pass teen era
